_meta.purpose: Pins each clinician-signed clinical field to its content hash + sign-off reference. scripts/check_signed_fields.py fails CI if a field's current value no longer matches its pinned hash, so a signed clinical field cannot change and merge without the manifest being updated (new hash + sign-off) in the same PR. Forcing function born from the 2026-07-10 trim-shipped-unconfirmed miss.
_meta.how_to_update: When a signed field legitimately changes: 1) get the clinician sign-off, 2) run `python scripts/check_signed_fields.py --generate` to read the new hash, 3) update sha256 + signed_by/signed_date/signoff here, in the SAME PR as the field change. Never update the hash without a real sign-off reference.
_meta.extensible: Add fields as they acquire sign-off records. Candidates not yet pinned: skill target_presentations (unaudited-keyword memory — 4 edits skipped sign-off; pinning them surfaces the gap for retroactive review or explicit grandfathering, silent is not an option), KB article .content (byte-for-byte, on T4 sign-off), P5 containment template (on T4 sign-off).
_meta.v2_scope: safety_rule_activation_map pins ACTIVATION state (active flag). Its mirror-image failure is #270's ~15 rules that are active WITHOUT sign-off (approved:false but running) — the opposite drift. v2 should pin APPROVAL state per rule alongside activation, so the manifest catches BOTH: this incident (signed-but-not-active) and #270 (active-but-not-signed). Owner: whoever holds #270. The two together make 'a safety rule's approval+activation state changed' impossible to merge unremarked.
